majorCities.name: Mazar-i-Sharif
majorCities.description: Home to the famous Blue Mosque

majorCities.name: Kandahar
majorCities.description: Cultural center of southern Afghanistan

majorCities.name: Jalalabad

Silk Road heritage
Traditional carpet weaving and calligraphy
Persian and Central Asian cultural blend
Rich oral poetry and storytelling traditions
transportation: Limited domestic air service
Basic road network with major highways

majorCities.name: Tirana
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, known for its colorful buildings and vibrant atmosphere.

majorCities.name: Durrës
majorCities.description: Albania's main port city, featuring an ancient amphitheater and beautiful beaches.

majorCities.name: Vlorë

majorCities.name: Shkodër
majorCities.description: Northern Albania's largest city, known for Rozafa Castle and close to Lake Shkodër.

Unique Albanian language (Shqip), an independent branch of Indo-European languages.
Strong tradition of hospitality, exemplified by the 'Besa' code of honor.
Rich folklore, traditional music, and vibrant traditional costumes.
Delicious cuisine blending Mediterranean and Ottoman flavors (e.g., Byrek, Tavë Kosi).
transportation: Extensive bus and 'furgon' (minibus) network for inter-city travel (informal schedules, cash payment).
Limited and outdated railway system, not widely used.
Car rental recommended for flexibility, especially to reach remote areas.
Taxis available, especially in urban areas and for shared rides.

Affordable travel destination compared to neighboring European countries.
Cash (Lek) is preferred, Euros also accepted in some tourist areas.

Albanians are generally very hospitable and friendly to visitors.
Majority Muslim population, but a secular country with religious tolerance.
Traffic rules can be erratic; be cautious when crossing roads.

majorCities.name: Algiers
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a major port and cultural center.

majorCities.name: Oran
majorCities.description: A significant coastal city in the west, known for its Spanish and French influences.

majorCities.name: Constantine
majorCities.description: The 'City of Bridges', a prominent city in the northeast with dramatic gorges.

majorCities.name: Annaba
majorCities.description: An important port city in the east, with Roman ruins nearby.

majorCities.name: Sétif
majorCities.description: A high-altitude city in eastern Algeria, known for its fountains and Roman ruins.

majorCities.name: Tlemcen
majorCities.description: A city with rich Moorish and Andalusian heritage in the northwest.

cultureHighlights: Rich blend of Berber, Arab, Ottoman, and French influences.
Traditional music genres like Raï and Chaâbi.
Vibrant handicrafts including pottery, carpets, and silver jewelry.
Emphasis on family and hospitality, especially sharing meals.
Strong oral storytelling traditions and poetry.
transportation: Extensive road network connecting major cities.
Limited but developing railway system.
Domestic flights connect major cities.
Shared taxis (clandestins or taxis collectifs) are common for inter-city travel.
Public buses available in urban areas.
goodToKnow: Largest country in Africa by land area, largely covered by the Sahara Desert.
Predominantly Muslim country.
Berber ethnic group has a significant cultural presence.
Historical sites include Roman ruins (Timgad, Djémila) and pre-historic cave paintings (Tassili n'Ajjer).
French is widely spoken, especially in urban areas and business.

majorCities.name: Pago Pago
majorCities.description: The de facto capital and largest town, located on Tutuila Island, featuring a deepwater harbor.

majorCities.name: Fagatogo
majorCities.description: The legislative capital, adjacent to Pago Pago, home to government buildings.

Traditional tattooing (tatau) is an important art form.
Fale (traditional open-sided houses) are common.
Samoan music and dance, including the siva and fa'ataupati.
Communal land ownership and strong family ties.
transportation: Limited road network, mostly on Tutuila Island.
Public transportation consists of colorful 'Aiga buses' (family buses) which run on flexible schedules.
Taxis are available.
Inter-island ferries and small planes connect the islands.
goodToKnow: Unincorporated territory of the United States.
Lush volcanic islands with rich marine biodiversity.
Experiences tropical climate with distinct wet and dry seasons.
Local currency is the US Dollar.
Fishing and tuna canning industry are significant to the economy.
National Park of American Samoa preserves rainforests, coral reefs, and Samoan culture.

majorCities.name: Andorra la Vella

Official language is Catalan, but Spanish, French, and Portuguese are also widely spoken.
Strong ties to both French and Spanish cultures, as well as distinct Pyrenean heritage.
Traditional festivals and folk dances are common, often with religious origins.
Cuisine blends Spanish and French influences, with mountain-specific dishes (e.g., trinxat).
transportation: No airports or railways; accessed by road from France or Spain.
Good internal road network, though mountainous terrain means winding roads.
Public bus system connects all major towns and ski resorts.
Taxis are available.
goodToKnow: Landlocked microstate in the Pyrenees mountains between France and Spain.
Popular for duty-free shopping and ski resorts.
High altitude and mountainous landscape.
Part of the Schengen Area, but not the European Union.
High quality of life and scenic beauty.

majorCities.name: Luanda
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a major port on the Atlantic coast.

majorCities.name: Huambo
majorCities.description: Second largest city, an important agricultural and industrial center in the highlands.

majorCities.name: Lobito
majorCities.description: A coastal city and major port in Benguela province, with a natural harbor.

majorCities.name: Benguela
majorCities.description: A historic coastal city known for its colonial architecture and proximity to beaches.

majorCities.name: Cabinda
majorCities.description: Exclave city, significant for its oil reserves, located north of the Congo River.

majorCities.name: Malanje
majorCities.description: A city in the north-central region, known for the Kalandula Falls nearby.

Strong influence of Portuguese colonial history on language, religion, and cuisine.
Vibrant music scene, including Kizomba and Semba (precursor to Samba).
Traditional art forms like masks and sculptures, often with spiritual significance.
Carnival celebrations are a significant cultural event.
transportation: Improving road network, but many roads outside major cities are unpaved.
Three main railway lines connect some inland areas to the coast.
Domestic flights are often the most reliable way to travel long distances.
Shared taxis (candongueiros) and private taxis are common in cities.
goodToKnow: Rich in natural resources, especially oil and diamonds.
Long Atlantic coastline with diverse landscapes, from deserts to tropical forests.
Still recovering from a long civil war; infrastructure is developing.
Visa requirements can be complex for visitors.
High cost of living, especially in Luanda.
Home to diverse wildlife, though national parks are still developing tourism infrastructure.

majorCities.name: The Valley
majorCities.description: The capital and main town, central to Anguilla's government and commerce.

Strong oral traditions, including storytelling and calypso music.
Boat racing is the national sport and a major cultural event.
Cuisine features fresh seafood, often grilled or stewed.
Friendly and laid-back island vibe.
transportation: Limited public transportation; taxis and rental cars are main options.
Ferries connect to neighboring St. Martin/Sint Maarten.
Small airport (Clayton J. Lloyd International Airport) for regional flights.
goodToKnow: British overseas territory in the Caribbean.
Known for its pristine white-sand beaches and turquoise waters.
Tourism is the primary industry.
Tropical climate with a hurricane season (June-November).
English is universally spoken.

majorCities.name: St. John's
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a major port and shopping hub with historic sites.

Cricket is the national sport and deeply ingrained in culture.

Calypso and Soca music are popular.
Local cuisine features fresh seafood, fungee, and pepperpot.

Taxis and rental cars are available.

Tropical climate with hurricane season (June-November).
Historical sites include Nelson's Dockyard.

majorCities.name: Buenos Aires

majorCities.name: Córdoba

Warm and welcoming people.

majorCities.name: Sydney

No public transportation network; taxis available in Stanley.
RAF Mount Pleasant serves as the main international airport, with limited commercial flights.
Small inter-island air service for remote settlements.
goodToKnow: Overseas territory of the United Kingdom in the South Atlantic Ocean.
Comprises two main islands (East and West Falkland) and numerous smaller ones.
Known for its unique wildlife, including five species of penguins, seals, and diverse birdlife.
Disputed territory claimed by Argentina.
Subpolar oceanic climate, often windy and cool.

majorCities.name: Suva
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, located on Viti Levu, a major port and administrative center.

majorCities.name: Nadi
majorCities.description: Major tourist hub on Viti Levu, gateway to resorts and islands.

majorCities.name: Lautoka
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, a major sugar cane processing center and port on Viti Levu.

cultureHighlights: Rich Indigenous Fijian culture (iTaukei) blended with Indian, European, and Chinese influences.
Strong emphasis on community, respect, and hospitality ('Bula!').
Traditional Kava ceremony is an important social ritual.
Known for its Meke (traditional dance) and Lali (slit drum) music.
Rugby is a national passion.
transportation: Well-developed road network on the main islands (Viti Levu, Vanua Levu).
Public buses are common and affordable.
Inter-island ferries and domestic flights connect the islands.
Nadi International Airport is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Island nation in Melanesia, part of Oceania, comprising over 300 islands (about 100 inhabited).
Known for its stunning natural beauty: pristine beaches, coral reefs, and lush rainforests.
Tourism is the main economic driver.
Experiences a tropical marine climate.
English is widely spoken, especially in tourist areas.

majorCities.name: Helsinki
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a vibrant coastal city known for its design, architecture, and Nordic charm.

majorCities.name: Espoo
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, part of the Helsinki metropolitan area, known for its natural beauty and tech companies.

majorCities.name: Tampere
majorCities.description: Third-largest city, located between two lakes, known for its industrial heritage and vibrant cultural scene.

majorCities.name: Vantaa
majorCities.description: Part of the Helsinki metropolitan area, home to Helsinki Airport.

majorCities.name: Oulu
majorCities.description: Major city in northern Finland, a technology and education hub near the Arctic Circle.

cultureHighlights: Known for 'Sisu' (grit, determination) and a deep connection to nature.
Sauna culture is fundamental to Finnish identity and well-being.
Strong tradition of design (e.g., Marimekko, Iittala) and innovation.
Love for quiet, personal space, and respecting nature.
Traditional folk music and Kalevala epic poetry.
transportation: Efficient public transport in cities (trams, buses, metro in Helsinki).
Well-maintained road network; winter tires are mandatory in winter.
Extensive railway system (VR) connecting major cities.
Helsinki-Vantaa Airport is the main international gateway.
Ferries connect to Sweden, Estonia, and Germany.
goodToKnow: Nordic country in Northern Europe, known as the 'Land of a Thousand Lakes' (actually 188,000+).
Vast forests, Arctic wilderness, and the Northern Lights in winter.
One of the happiest countries in the world with a high standard of living and excellent education system.
Experiences long daylight hours in summer and long dark periods in winter.
Santa Claus's official home is said to be in Lapland.

majorCities.name: Papeete
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, located on Tahiti island, a vibrant hub and main port of entry.

majorCities.name: Faaa
majorCities.description: Adjacent to Papeete on Tahiti, home to the international airport.

cultureHighlights: Rich Polynesian culture with a focus on 'Mana' (spiritual power) and connection to nature.
Traditional music (ukulele, pahu drums) and dance (tamure) are vibrant.
Known for its tattooing traditions and vibrant pareos (sarongs).
Emphasis on hospitality and leisurely island life.
Black pearls are a significant cultural and economic product.
transportation: Well-maintained roads on the main islands; rental cars and scooters are popular.
Inter-island flights are essential for connecting the different archipelagos.
Ferries connect Tahiti to Moorea.
Faa'a International Airport (Tahiti) is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Overseas collectivity of France in the South Pacific Ocean.
Comprises several groups of Polynesian islands, including Tahiti, Bora Bora, and Moorea.
Known for its stunning volcanic landscapes, turquoise lagoons, and luxury resorts.
Tourism is the main economic driver.
Tropical climate with warm temperatures year-round.

majorCities.name: Paris
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a global center for art, fashion, gastronomy, and culture.

majorCities.name: Marseille
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, a major port on the Mediterranean, known for its diverse culture and history.

majorCities.name: Lyon
majorCities.description: Third-largest city, a gastronomic capital and UNESCO World Heritage site with Roman ruins.

majorCities.name: Toulouse
majorCities.description: Known as 'La Ville Rose' (The Pink City), a major aerospace and university center.

majorCities.name: Nice
majorCities.description: Major city on the French Riviera, famous for its Promenade des Anglais and vibrant arts scene.

majorCities.name: Nantes
majorCities.description: Historic city in western France, known for its Dukes of Brittany castle and artistic innovation.

majorCities.name: Strasbourg
majorCities.description: City in Alsace, seat of the European Parliament, known for its Gothic cathedral and German influence.

majorCities.name: Montpellier
majorCities.description: Fast-growing city in Southern France, known for its vibrant student life and historic center.

majorCities.name: Bordeaux
majorCities.description: World wine capital, known for its elegant architecture and proximity to famous vineyards.

Strong emphasis on intellectual discourse, philosophy, and 'savoir-vivre' (art of living).
Numerous architectural masterpieces from Roman to Gothic and modern eras.
Rich history of Enlightenment and revolutionary movements.
Celebrates a wide range of national holidays and regional festivals.
transportation: Extensive and efficient high-speed rail network (TGV) connecting major cities.
Well-developed road and highway network.
Efficient public transport in cities (metro, trams, buses).
Paris Charles de Gaulle Airport and Orly Airport are major international hubs, along with other regional airports.
Ferries connect to UK and other Mediterranean countries.
goodToKnow: Located in Western Europe, with coastlines on the Atlantic Ocean and Mediterranean Sea.
Diverse geography, including mountains (Alps, Pyrenees), rivers, and coastal plains.
World's most visited country, famous for its landmarks, museums, and gastronomy.
Member of the European Union, NATO, and a permanent member of the UN Security Council.
Temperate climate with regional variations.

majorCities.name: Libreville
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a major port and economic hub on the Komo Estuary.

majorCities.description: Second-largest city, a major oil and timber center on the coast.

majorCities.name: Franceville

Traditional mask-making and wood carving are prominent art forms.

Traditional music and dance play a central role in ceremonies.
Cuisine features local staples like cassava, plantains, and various stews.

Tropical climate with a wet and dry season.

majorCities.name: Tbilisi
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, known for its diverse architecture, ancient churches, and vibrant old town.

majorCities.name: Batumi

Known for its polyphonic singing, a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage.
Strong tradition of hospitality and feasting (Supra).
Orthodox Christian heritage with numerous ancient churches and monasteries.

Railway system connects major cities, including Tbilisi to Batumi.
Public transport (metro, buses, marshrutkas) available in Tbilisi.
Shota Rustaveli Tbilisi International Airport is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Transcontinental country at the crossroads of Europe and Asia, located in the Caucasus region.
Known for its stunning mountainous landscapes (Caucasus Mountains), Black Sea coastline, and ancient cave cities.

Developing tourism industry.
Experiences diverse climates from subtropical to alpine.

majorCities.name: Berlin
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a hub of art, history, fashion, and nightlife, with iconic landmarks like the Brandenburg Gate.

majorCities.name: Hamburg
majorCities.description: Major port city in northern Germany, known for its extensive harbor, vibrant nightlife, and Speicherstadt.

majorCities.name: Munich
majorCities.description: Capital of Bavaria, famous for Oktoberfest, beautiful architecture, and beer gardens.

majorCities.name: Köln
majorCities.description: Rhine River city, known for its magnificent Cologne Cathedral and Roman history.

majorCities.name: Frankfurt
majorCities.description: Major financial hub, known for its modern skyline and international trade fairs.

majorCities.name: Stuttgart
majorCities.description: Capital of Baden-Württemberg, home to Mercedes-Benz and Porsche museums.

majorCities.name: Düsseldorf
majorCities.description: Fashion and art city on the Rhine, known for its Königsallee shopping street.

majorCities.name: Leipzig

World-renowned Autobahn network (sections without speed limits).
Extensive high-speed rail network (ICE) connecting all major cities.

goodToKnow: Located in Central Europe, the most populous country in the European Union.
Diverse landscapes from North Sea and Baltic Sea coasts to the Alps in the south.
Leading global economy and major industrial power.
Known for its technological innovation and strong social welfare system.
Temperate seasonal climate.

majorCities.name: Accra

majorCities.name: Athens

GGY.currency: Guernsey Pound (GGP), British Pound (GBP) also accepted

Home to Issyk-Kul Lake, one of the world's largest alpine lakes.
Experiences a continental climate with hot summers and cold winters.
Developing tourism industry focused on ecotourism and mountain adventures.

majorCities.name: Vientiane
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, located on the Mekong River, known for its temples and French colonial architecture.

majorCities.name: Luang Prabang
majorCities.description: Ancient royal capital and UNESCO World Heritage site, known for its Buddhist temples and serene atmosphere.

majorCities.name: Savannakhet
majorCities.description: Major city in southern Laos, an important trade hub on the Mekong River.

cultureHighlights: Strong Buddhist traditions influencing daily life, art, and architecture.
Traditional music and dance (Lam Vong) are central to celebrations.
Known for its silk weaving and handicrafts.
Emphasis on humility, respect, and 'Boun' (festivals).
Cuisine features sticky rice, fresh herbs, and spicy dishes (e.g., Laap).
transportation: Road network is improving but can be challenging, especially in mountainous areas.
High-speed railway connects Vientiane to China, with stops including Luang Prabang.
River transport on the Mekong is crucial.
Wattay International Airport (Vientiane) and Luang Prabang International Airport are main international gateways.
goodToKnow: Landlocked country in Southeast Asia, traversed by the Mekong River.
Known for its mountainous terrain, lush landscapes, and ancient Buddhist temples.
One of the least densely populated countries in Southeast Asia.
Experiences a tropical monsoon climate with distinct wet and dry seasons.
Socialist republic, with a rapidly developing economy.

majorCities.name: Riga
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a UNESCO World Heritage site known for its Art Nouveau architecture and medieval Old Town.

majorCities.name: Daugavpils
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, located in southeastern Latvia, known for its fortress.

majorCities.name: Liepāja
majorCities.description: Major port city on the Baltic Sea, known for its beach and music scene.

cultureHighlights: Rich history with influences from Germanic, Slavic, and Scandinavian cultures.
Strong folk music (Dainas) and dance traditions, especially during Jāņi (Midsummer).
Known for its wooden architecture and Art Nouveau buildings.
Amber is a significant cultural material.
Emphasis on nature, forests, and the Baltic Sea.
transportation: Well-developed road network.
Extensive railway system connects major cities.
Efficient public transport in cities (trams, buses, trolleybuses).
Riga International Airport is the main international gateway.
Ferries connect to Sweden and Germany.
goodToKnow: Located in the Baltic region of Northern Europe, with a Baltic Sea coastline.
One of the three Baltic states.
Known for its vast forests, numerous lakes, and coastal dunes.
Member of the European Union, NATO, and the Eurozone.
Temperate seasonal climate.

majorCities.name: Beirut
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a vibrant Mediterranean city with a rich history and resilient spirit.

majorCities.name: Tripoli
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, a historic port city in northern Lebanon.

majorCities.name: Sidon
majorCities.description: Ancient Phoenician city on the southern coast, known for its sea castle and souqs.

majorCities.name: Tyre
majorCities.description: Historic Phoenician city and UNESCO World Heritage site on the southern coast.

cultureHighlights: Rich history influenced by Phoenician, Roman, Arab, Ottoman, and French cultures.
Blend of Christian and Muslim communities.
Vibrant music scene (Arabic pop, traditional folk) and dance.
Renowned for its cuisine (Mezze, tabbouleh, kibbeh) and hospitality.
Emphasis on fashion, art, and intellectual life, especially in Beirut.
transportation: Road network connects major cities; quality varies.
No railway system.
Shared taxis (serviis) and private taxis are common.
Beirut-Rafic Hariri International Airport is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Located in Western Asia, on the eastern shore of the Mediterranean Sea.
Known for its ancient archaeological sites, beautiful coastline, and mountainous interior (skiing in winter).
Faces significant economic and political challenges.
Often called the 'Switzerland of the Middle East' or 'Paris of the Middle East'.
Mediterranean climate, with hot, dry summers and mild, wet winters.

LSO.currency: Lesotho Loti (LSL), South African Rand (ZAR) also accepted

majorCities.name: Maseru
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, located on the Caledon River.

cultureHighlights: Strong Basotho culture, known for its traditional blankets and conical hats (mokorotlo).
Traditional music and dance, often accompanied by local instruments.
Emphasis on community, respect for elders, and traditional leadership.
Horse riding is a significant part of rural life and culture.
Staple food is papa (corn porridge) and various stews.
transportation: Limited road network, some major roads are paved but mountainous terrain makes travel challenging.
No railways.
Minibus taxis are common for local and inter-city transport.
Moshoeshoe I International Airport (Maseru) is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Landlocked country in Southern Africa, an enclave entirely surrounded by South Africa.
Known as the 'Kingdom in the Sky' due to its high altitude and mountainous terrain.
One of Africa's poorest countries, facing challenges with poverty and HIV/AIDS.
Snowfall occurs in the highlands during winter.
Unique landscape with waterfalls and traditional villages.

majorCities.name: Monrovia
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a major port on the Atlantic coast.

majorCities.name: Gbarnga
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, located in Bong County, an important agricultural center.

cultureHighlights: Unique history as Africa's first independent republic, founded by freed American slaves.
Blend of African American and Indigenous West African cultures.
Traditional music and dance forms vary by ethnic group.
Strong oral traditions and storytelling.
Cuisine features rice as a staple, with various stews and spicy dishes.
transportation: Limited and often poorly maintained road network; travel can be challenging, especially during rainy season.
No railway system for passengers.
Motorcycle taxis and shared taxis (kekehs) are common.
Roberts International Airport (Monrovia) is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Located in West Africa, with a long Atlantic coastline.
Rich in natural resources (iron ore, diamonds, gold, timber).
Still recovering from civil wars; faces challenges with infrastructure and poverty.
Much of the country is covered by dense rainforest.
Tropical climate with a distinct wet and dry season.

majorCities.name: Tripoli
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a historic port city on the Mediterranean coast.

majorCities.name: Benghazi
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, a major port and economic center in eastern Libya.

majorCities.name: Misratah
majorCities.description: Coastal city and major port, an important industrial and commercial hub.

majorCities.name: Zliten
majorCities.description: Coastal city known for its university and historical sites.

cultureHighlights: Rich history influenced by Berber, Roman, Arab, Ottoman, and Italian cultures.
Strong Islamic and Arab traditions.
Bedouin heritage is prominent, especially in desert areas.
Traditional music and dance are common.
Cuisine features Mediterranean and North African influences (e.g., couscous, bazin).
transportation: Road network connects major cities, but conditions and safety vary significantly due to ongoing conflict.
No operational railway system.
Domestic flights are often restricted and unreliable.
Tripoli International Airport and Benghazi Benina Airport (operational status varies).
goodToKnow: Located in North Africa, with a long Mediterranean coastline.
Vast majority of the country is covered by the Sahara Desert.
Rich in oil reserves, which dominate its economy.
Faces ongoing political instability and armed conflict, making travel highly dangerous.
Known for its ancient Roman ruins (Leptis Magna, Sabratha).

majorCities.name: Vaduz
majorCities.description: The capital and seat of the Prince, known for its castle and art museum.

cultureHighlights: Unique identity as a small, independent principality.
Blend of Alemannic (German-speaking) and Alpine traditions.
Strong emphasis on tradition, community, and quality of life.
Known for its philately (stamp collecting) and wine production.
Winter sports are a popular pastime.
transportation: Well-maintained road network; cars are common.
Public bus system connects all municipalities.
No airport; closest major airports are in Zurich (Switzerland) and Friedrichshafen (Germany).
No internal railway, but a short international rail line passes through.
goodToKnow: Landlocked microstate in the Alps, between Switzerland and Austria.
One of the smallest countries in the world and one of the richest by GDP per capita.
Constitutional monarchy with a reigning Prince.
Known for its stunning mountain landscapes and medieval castles.
Alpine climate with cold winters and mild summers.

majorCities.name: Vilnius
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a UNESCO World Heritage site known for its Baroque Old Town.

majorCities.name: Kaunas
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, a historic city with a strong basketball culture.

majorCities.name: Klaipėda
majorCities.description: Major port city on the Baltic Sea, known for its German heritage and maritime traditions.

majorCities.name: Šiauliai
majorCities.description: Fourth-largest city, known for the Hill of Crosses nearby.

cultureHighlights: Rich history as a powerful Grand Duchy, influencing Eastern Europe.
Unique Baltic language (Lithuanian) with ancient roots.
Strong folk music and dance traditions, especially during Midsummer (Joninės).
Known for its amber jewelry and linen textiles.
Emphasis on nature, forests, and lakes.
transportation: Well-developed road network.
Extensive railway system connects major cities.
Efficient public transport in cities (buses, trolleybuses).
Vilnius International Airport is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Located in the Baltic region of Northern Europe, with a Baltic Sea coastline.
One of the three Baltic states.
Known for its vast forests, numerous lakes, and the Curonian Spit (UNESCO site).
Member of the European Union, NATO, and the Eurozone.
Temperate seasonal climate.

majorCities.name: Lilongwe
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, an administrative and commercial hub in central Malawi.

majorCities.name: Blantyre
majorCities.description: Second-largest city and main economic center, located in southern Malawi.

majorCities.name: Mzuzu
majorCities.description: Third-largest city, a growing urban center in northern Malawi.

cultureHighlights: Known as the 'Warm Heart of Africa' due to its friendly and welcoming people.
Diverse ethnic groups with rich traditions of music, dance, and storytelling.
Chewa people are known for their Gule Wamkulu secret society dances.
Traditional crafts include wood carving and basketry.
Strong emphasis on family and community.
transportation: Limited paved road network; many roads are unpaved, especially in rural areas.
No significant passenger railway system.
Minibuses (matolas) are common for public transport.
Kamuzu International Airport (Lilongwe) and Chileka International Airport (Blantyre) are main international gateways.
goodToKnow: Landlocked country in Southeast Africa.
Dominated by Lake Malawi, which covers about a third of its area and is a UNESCO World Heritage site.
One of the world's least developed countries, facing challenges with poverty and food security.
Known for its beautiful scenery and wildlife (safaris).
Tropical climate with wet and dry seasons.

majorCities.name: Kuala Lumpur
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a modern metropolis known for its Petronas Towers and diverse culture.

majorCities.name: George Town
majorCities.description: Historic city on Penang Island, a UNESCO World Heritage site known for its colonial architecture and street art.

majorCities.name: Johor Bahru
majorCities.description: Major city in the south, gateway to Singapore and a growing economic hub.

majorCities.name: Ipoh
majorCities.description: City in Perak, known for its colonial architecture, cave temples, and white coffee.

majorCities.name: Shah Alam
majorCities.description: Capital of Selangor, known for its Blue Mosque and modern urban planning.

majorCities.name: Kuching
majorCities.description: Capital of Sarawak on Borneo island, known for its riverside charm and cultural diversity.

majorCities.name: Kota Kinabalu
majorCities.description: Capital of Sabah on Borneo island, gateway to Mount Kinabalu and tropical islands.

cultureHighlights: Highly diverse multi-ethnic and multicultural society (Malay, Chinese, Indian, Indigenous).
Blend of Malay, Islamic, Chinese, Indian, and indigenous cultures.
Vibrant traditional music (Gamelan, kompang) and dance forms.
Renowned for its delicious and diverse street food.
Celebrates numerous religious and cultural festivals throughout the year.
transportation: Well-developed road network, including expressways connecting major cities.
Efficient public transport in Kuala Lumpur (LRT, MRT, Monorail).
Intercity rail services connect some parts of Peninsular Malaysia.
Kuala Lumpur International Airport is the main international gateway.
Ferries connect Peninsular Malaysia to islands and Borneo island via domestic flights.
goodToKnow: Located in Southeast Asia, comprising Peninsular Malaysia and East Malaysia (on Borneo island).
Known for its beautiful beaches, rainforests, and diverse wildlife.
Tropical climate with high humidity and rainfall year-round.
Constitutional monarchy with a unique rotating kingship system.
Developing economy, driven by manufacturing, agriculture, and tourism.

majorCities.name: Malé
majorCities.description: The capital and most populous city, located on a tiny island, a bustling hub and main entry point.

cultureHighlights: Unique blend of South Asian, Middle Eastern, and African influences due to trade routes.
Islam is the official religion and central to daily life.
Traditional music (Bodu Beru) and dance.
Emphasis on community and maritime traditions (fishing).
Cuisine features fresh seafood, coconut, and spicy flavors.
transportation: Limited roads on individual islands; walking, cycling, and scooters are common.
Speedboats, seaplanes, and domestic flights are essential for inter-island travel.
Velana International Airport (Hulhulé Island, near Malé) is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Island nation in the Indian Ocean, consisting of 26 natural atolls and over 1,000 coral islands.
Known for its stunning overwater bungalows, white sand beaches, and vibrant coral reefs.
Lowest country in the world, highly vulnerable to rising sea levels.
Tourism is the primary industry.
Tropical monsoon climate with two main seasons.

majorCities.name: Bamako
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a bustling port on the Niger River.

majorCities.name: Sikasso
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, an important agricultural center in southern Mali.

majorCities.name: Mopti
majorCities.description: Port city on the Niger River, known as the 'Venice of Mali' and a major fishing hub.

cultureHighlights: Rich history of ancient West African empires (Mali, Songhai).
Known for its vibrant music (Mande music) and griots (hereditary storytellers/musicians).
Traditional mud-brick architecture (e.g., Great Mosque of Djenné).
Emphasis on hospitality and community values.
Islam is the predominant religion.
transportation: Limited paved road network; many roads are unpaved and difficult to navigate, especially in the north.
Limited railway system.
River transport on the Niger River is crucial.
Bamako-Sénou International Airport is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Landlocked country in West Africa, with a large portion in the Sahara Desert.
Struggles with political instability, security challenges, and poverty.
Rich in natural resources, especially gold.
Home to historical cities like Timbuktu and Djenné, UNESCO World Heritage sites.
Sahelian and tropical climate zones.

majorCities.name: Valletta
majorCities.description: The capital city, a UNESCO World Heritage site and fortified city known for its Baroque architecture.

majorCities.name: Birkirkara
majorCities.description: Largest town on Malta by population, a busy commercial and residential center.

cultureHighlights: Rich history influenced by Phoenician, Roman, Arab, Norman, and British cultures.
Unique Maltese language, a Semitic language with Romance and English influences.
Strong Catholic traditions and numerous religious feasts.
Known for its vibrant village festas (festivals).
Traditional Maltese food features Mediterranean and North African flavors.
transportation: Extensive public bus system covering the islands.
No railways.
Rental cars are common; driving is on the left.
Malta International Airport is the only airport.
Ferries connect the islands of Malta and Gozo.
goodToKnow: Archipelagic island nation in the central Mediterranean Sea.
One of the smallest and most densely populated countries in the world.
Known for its ancient megalithic temples, stunning coastline, and historic cities.
Popular tourist destination with a warm, sunny climate.
Member of the European Union and the Eurozone.

majorCities.name: Majuro
majorCities.description: The capital and largest urban area, located on Majuro Atoll, home to most of the population.

cultureHighlights: Rich Micronesian culture, adapted to atoll living and seafaring.
Traditional stick charts (rebbelib) for navigation are unique.
Known for its traditional weaving (baskets, mats) and handicrafts.
Emphasis on community, extended family, and traditional leadership (Iroij).
Oral traditions and storytelling are important.
transportation: Limited roads on the main atolls; walking and cycling are common.
Small boats and domestic flights connect the islands.
Marshall Islands International Airport (Majuro) is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Island nation in Micronesia, Western Pacific, comprising 29 atolls and 5 islands.
Faces significant challenges from rising sea levels and climate change.
History includes being a US nuclear testing site (Bikini Atoll).
Economy relies on US aid, fishing, and copra production.
Tropical marine climate.

majorCities.name: Nouakchott
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, located on the Atlantic coast, a major economic and administrative center.

majorCities.name: Nouadhibou
majorCities.description: Second-largest city and major port in the north, important for fishing and iron ore exports.

majorCities.name: Kiffa
majorCities.description: Major town in the south, a traditional stopping point in the desert.

cultureHighlights: Rich blend of Arab and Berber cultures, with influences from sub-Saharan Africa.
Islam is the state religion and central to daily life.
Traditional music features the tidinit (lute) and ardin (harp).
Nomadic heritage is still present in some communities.
Emphasis on hospitality, poetry, and storytelling.
transportation: Limited paved road network; many roads are unpaved, especially in desert areas.
World's longest train (iron ore train) runs across the desert, sometimes carrying passengers.
Bush taxis and minibuses are common for inter-city travel.
Nouakchott–Oumtounsy International Airport is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Located in West Africa, with a long Atlantic coastline, mostly covered by the Sahara Desert.
Rich in natural resources, especially iron ore and recently offshore gas.
Faces challenges with desertification, poverty, and political stability.
Ancient caravan cities (e.g., Chinguetti, Ouadane) are UNESCO World Heritage sites.
Hot, arid climate.

majorCities.name: Port Louis
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a major port and economic center with a vibrant waterfront.

majorCities.name: Beau Bassin-Rose Hill
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, a major residential and commercial area in the central plateau.

majorCities.name: Vacoas-Phoenix
majorCities.description: Third-largest city, also located in the central plateau.

cultureHighlights: Highly diverse multicultural society with influences from Indian, African, French, Chinese, and British heritage.
Mauritian Creole is the most commonly spoken language.
Sega music and dance are distinctive to Mauritius.
Numerous religious and cultural festivals celebrated throughout the year.
Cuisine is a flavorful blend of Indian, Chinese, African, and French influences.
transportation: Well-developed road network covering the island.
Efficient public bus system.
No railways.
Sir Seewoosagur Ramgoolam International Airport is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Island nation in the Indian Ocean, east of Madagascar.
Known for its stunning volcanic landscapes, coral reefs, and pristine beaches.
One of Africa's most developed and stable democracies.
Major luxury tourist destination.
Tropical climate with warm temperatures year-round.

majorCities.name: Mexico City
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a sprawling metropolis with ancient Aztec ruins, colonial architecture, and vibrant culture.

majorCities.name: Guadalajara
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, known as the birthplace of Mariachi music and tequila.

majorCities.name: Monterrey
majorCities.description: Major industrial and business hub in northern Mexico, surrounded by mountains.

majorCities.name: Puebla
majorCities.description: Historic colonial city, a UNESCO World Heritage site known for its Talavera pottery and culinary traditions.

majorCities.name: Tijuana
majorCities.description: Border city with the U.S., a major manufacturing and cultural hub.

majorCities.name: León
majorCities.description: Major city in Guanajuato, known for its leather industry and shoes.

majorCities.name: Ciudad Juárez
majorCities.description: Major border city with the U.S. (El Paso, Texas), an important industrial center.

majorCities.name: Zapopan
majorCities.description: Part of the Guadalajara metropolitan area, known for its basilica.

majorCities.name: Mexicali
majorCities.description: Capital of Baja California, a major border city and agricultural center.

majorCities.name: Aguascalientes
majorCities.description: Historic city in central Mexico, known for its colonial architecture and San Marcos Fair.

cultureHighlights: Rich blend of Indigenous (Maya, Aztec) and Spanish colonial heritage.
Known for its vibrant festivals (Day of the Dead, Grito de Dolores), Mariachi music, and Folkloric dance.
UNESCO World Heritage sites abound, from ancient ruins to colonial cities.
World-renowned cuisine (Taco, Mole, Tamales) recognized by UNESCO.
Strong emphasis on family, community, and hospitality.
transportation: Extensive road network, including toll highways (autopistas) connecting major cities.
Developing high-speed rail projects; long-distance buses are a common and efficient mode of transport.
Mexico City Metro is extensive; taxis and ride-sharing are common in cities.
Mexico City International Airport, Cancun International Airport, and Guadalajara International Airport are major international gateways.
goodToKnow: Located in North America, with long coastlines on the Pacific Ocean and the Gulf of Mexico/Caribbean Sea.
Diverse geography, including deserts, mountains, rainforests, and beaches.
One of the world's most biodiverse countries.
Rich history of ancient civilizations (Maya, Aztec, Olmec) and colonial periods.
Varies from tropical to desert climates depending on region.

majorCities.name: Palikir
majorCities.description: The capital, a small town on Pohnpei island.

majorCities.name: Weno
majorCities.description: Largest town in Chuuk State, a major population center.

cultureHighlights: Diverse Micronesian cultures across four states (Yap, Chuuk, Pohnpei, Kosrae).
Traditional navigation skills and canoe building are highly valued.
Known for its intricate weaving, wood carving, and traditional attire.
Strong emphasis on traditional leadership and communal living.
Oral traditions and storytelling are central to cultural transmission.
transportation: Limited road networks on the main islands; most travel is within the island.
Inter-island travel is primarily by domestic flights and occasional cargo ships.
Pohnpei International Airport is the main international gateway, with other smaller international airports on other states.
goodToKnow: Island nation in Micronesia, Western Pacific, comprising four main island states.
Known for its stunning coral reefs, diverse marine life, and WWII wreck diving.
Receives significant financial assistance from the United States.
Faces challenges from rising sea levels and climate change.
Tropical marine climate year-round.

majorCities.name: Chișinău
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a green city known for its Soviet-era architecture and parks.

majorCities.name: Tiraspol
majorCities.description: De facto capital of Transnistria (unrecognized breakaway region), located on the Dniester River.

majorCities.name: Bălţi
majorCities.description: Second-largest city in the north, an important industrial and cultural center.

cultureHighlights: Blend of Romanian, Russian, and Ukrainian influences.
Known for its rich winemaking traditions and vineyards.
Traditional folk music and dance are vibrant.
Strong emphasis on hospitality and family gatherings.
Orthodox Christian heritage with numerous monasteries.
transportation: Road network connects major towns; quality varies.
Railway system exists but is slow.
Public buses and trolleybuses are common in cities.
Chișinău International Airport is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Landlocked country in Eastern Europe, between Romania and Ukraine.
One of Europe's least visited countries.
Known for its fertile plains and rolling hills, ideal for agriculture.
Faces challenges with poverty and political stability, including the Transnistrian conflict.
Continental climate with warm summers and cold winters.

majorCities.name: Monaco
majorCities.description: The historic Old Town, perched on a rock, home to the Prince's Palace.

majorCities.name: Monte-Carlo
majorCities.description: The most famous quarter, known for its casino, luxury resorts, and Grand Prix circuit.

cultureHighlights: Blend of French, Italian, and Monegasque influences.
Known for its glamour, luxury, and high-profile events (F1 Grand Prix, Yacht Show).
Monegasque dialect (a Ligurian dialect of Italian) is spoken by some locals.
Strong emphasis on tradition and loyalty to the princely family.
Classical music and ballet are highly valued.
transportation: Dense and efficient road network, often with tunnels.
Local bus system and elevators/escalators connect different levels of the city.
No airport in Monaco itself; Nice Côte d'Azur Airport (France) is the closest.
Monte Carlo railway station provides train connections along the French Riviera.
goodToKnow: Sovereign city-state and microstate on the French Riviera, bordered by France on three sides and the Mediterranean Sea.
Second smallest country in the world by area.
Known for its tax haven status, luxurious lifestyle, and gambling.
Constitutional monarchy with a hereditary Prince.
Mediterranean climate with mild, wet winters and hot, dry summers.

majorCities.name: Ulaanbaatar
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a blend of traditional nomadic and Soviet-era architecture, with modern high-rises.

cultureHighlights: Strong nomadic culture and heritage from Genghis Khan's empire.
Traditional throat singing (khoomei) and horse-head fiddle (morin khuur).
Naadam Festival (July) is a major celebration of wrestling, archery, and horse racing.
Buddhism (Tibetan Vajrayana) is the predominant religion.
Gers (yurts) are traditional dwellings and symbols of nomadic life.
transportation: Road network connects major towns, but many are unpaved, especially outside Ulaanbaatar.
Trans-Mongolian Railway connects Ulaanbaatar to Russia and China.
Domestic flights are available to some provincial centers.
Chinggis Khaan International Airport (Ulaanbaatar) is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Landlocked country in East Asia, between Russia and China.
Vast steppes, Gobi Desert, and Altai Mountains dominate its landscape.
One of the least densely populated countries in the world.
Known for its nomadic traditions, equestrian culture, and pristine wilderness.
Extreme continental climate with long, cold winters and short, hot summers.

majorCities.name: Podgorica
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a modern city rebuilt after WWII, surrounded by rivers and mountains.

majorCities.name: Nikšić
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, an industrial center in the central region.

majorCities.name: Herceg Novi
majorCities.description: Coastal town in the Bay of Kotor, known for its fortresses and Mimosa Festival.

majorCities.name: Budva
majorCities.description: Popular coastal town on the Adriatic, known for its medieval Old Town and beaches.

cultureHighlights: Rich history with influences from Ottoman, Venetian, and Austro-Hungarian empires.
Strong folk music and dance traditions.
Orthodox Christian heritage, with numerous monasteries.
Emphasis on hospitality and strong family ties.
Cuisine features Mediterranean and Balkan influences.
transportation: Road network connects major cities, but mountainous terrain means winding roads.
Railway connects Podgorica to Belgrade (Serbia) and Bar (coast).
Podgorica Airport and Tivat Airport are the main international gateways.
Buses are common for inter-city travel.
goodToKnow: Located in the Balkans, with a beautiful Adriatic coastline.
Known for its dramatic mountainous landscapes, deep canyons, and coastal resorts.
One of Europe's youngest independent states (since 2006).
Developing tourism industry.
Mediterranean climate along the coast, continental inland.

majorCities.name: Brades
majorCities.description: The current de facto capital and largest settlement, located in the safe zone after the volcanic eruptions.

cultureHighlights: Blend of African, Irish, and British influences.
Known as the 'Emerald Isle of the Caribbean' due to its lush green landscape and Irish heritage.
St. Patrick's Day is a major public holiday, unique to the Caribbean.
Traditional music and dance forms are preserved.
Resilient community adapting to volcanic challenges.
transportation: Limited road network, mostly in the safe northern zone.
No public buses; taxis are available.
John A. Osborne Airport is the main air gateway (small planes only).
Ferries connect to Antigua.
goodToKnow: British overseas territory in the Caribbean, part of the Leeward Islands.
Dominated by an active volcano (Soufrière Hills), which destroyed the former capital Plymouth.
Much of the island is an exclusion zone due to volcanic activity.
Developing a new capital at Little Bay.
Tropical climate with hurricane season (June-November).

majorCities.name: Casablanca
majorCities.description: Largest city and economic hub, a major port and modern metropolis.

majorCities.name: Fes
majorCities.description: Ancient imperial city and UNESCO World Heritage site, known for its tanneries and medina.

majorCities.name: Tangier
majorCities.description: Major port city on the Strait of Gibraltar, a historic gateway between Africa and Europe.

majorCities.name: Marrakesh
majorCities.description: Imperial city, known for its vibrant souqs, Jemaa el-Fna square, and stunning gardens.

majorCities.name: Rabat

majorCities.name: Namangan
majorCities.description: Major city in the Fergana Valley, an important industrial and agricultural center.

majorCities.name: Andijon
majorCities.description: City in the Fergana Valley, known as a major industrial city.

majorCities.name: Bukhara
majorCities.description: Ancient Silk Road city and UNESCO World Heritage site, known for its well-preserved medieval architecture.

majorCities.name: Urganch
majorCities.description: City in western Uzbekistan, gateway to the ancient city of Khiva.

cultureHighlights: Rich history as a crossroads of ancient Silk Road civilizations (Persian, Turkic, Mongol).
Known for its vibrant Islamic architecture, intricate tile work, and traditional handicrafts.
Traditional music (maqom) and classical dance forms.
Emphasis on hospitality, family, and community gatherings (mahalla).
Plov (rice pilaf) is the national dish, a central part of Uzbek cuisine.
transportation: Well-developed road network connects major cities.
Extensive railway system, including a high-speed rail (Afrosiyob) connecting Tashkent, Samarkand, and Bukhara.
Tashkent Metro is efficient for urban transport.
Tashkent International Airport is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Double-landlocked country in Central Asia, meaning it's surrounded by other landlocked countries.
Home to some of the most iconic Silk Road cities (Samarkand, Bukhara, Khiva).
Largest population in Central Asia.
Former Soviet Republic, gained independence in 1991.
Continental climate with hot summers and cold winters.

majorCities.name: Singapore
majorCities.description: A global financial hub, modern city-state known for its efficiency, green spaces, and diverse culinary scene.

cultureHighlights: Highly multicultural society with influences from Malay, Chinese, Indian, and Western cultures.
Known for its hawker (street food) culture, a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage.
Emphasis on efficiency, cleanliness, and meritocracy.
Vibrant arts scene with diverse performing arts.
Strong blend of traditional values and modern innovation.
transportation: Excellent and comprehensive public transport system (MRT, buses).
Well-maintained road network; strict traffic laws.
Changi Airport is a major global aviation hub, consistently ranked among the best in the world.
Taxis and ride-sharing are widely available.
goodToKnow: Island city-state in Southeast Asia, located at the southern tip of the Malay Peninsula.
One of the world's leading financial centers and a major global shipping hub.
Highly urbanized with a significant amount of reclaimed land.
Known for its strict laws and high standard of living.
Tropical rainforest climate with no distinct seasons.

SXM.currency: Netherlands Antillean Guilder (ANG), USD widely accepted

majorCities.name: Philipsburg
majorCities.description: The capital and main town on the Dutch side, known for its duty-free shopping and cruise ship port.

cultureHighlights: Unique blend of Dutch, French, and Caribbean cultures (shared island with French Saint Martin).
Vibrant music (calypso, soca) and dance scene.
Known for its lively nightlife and diverse culinary offerings.
Emphasis on tourism and hospitality.
Carnival is a major annual event.
transportation: Well-developed road network; rental cars and scooters are popular.
Public buses are available; taxis are common.
Princess Juliana International Airport (Sint Maarten) is a major regional air hub.
Ferries connect to neighboring islands like Saba and St. Barths.
goodToKnow: Constituent country of the Kingdom of the Netherlands, occupying the southern part of the island of Saint Martin.
Known for its beautiful beaches, lively resorts, and duty-free shopping.
Experiences a tropical climate with a hurricane season (June-November).
The island is famous for Maho Beach, where planes land directly over the beach.
Divided into Dutch (Sint Maarten) and French (Saint-Martin) sides.

majorCities.name: Bratislava
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, located on the Danube River, known for its historic Old Town and castle.

majorCities.name: Košice
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, a major cultural and industrial center in eastern Slovakia.

majorCities.name: Prešov
majorCities.description: Third-largest city, known for its salt mines and historic architecture.

majorCities.name: Žilina
majorCities.description: City in northern Slovakia, an important industrial and university hub.

cultureHighlights: Rich Slavic heritage with influences from Austro-Hungarian and Czech cultures.
Traditional folk music and dance forms (čardáš).
Known for its beautiful castles, wooden churches, and folk architecture.
Emphasis on hospitality, family, and traditional cuisine (bryndzové halušky).
Strong Catholic traditions and numerous religious festivals.
transportation: Well-developed road network, including motorways.
Extensive railway system connects major cities.
Efficient public transport in cities (trams, buses, trolleybuses).
Bratislava Airport is the main international gateway, with other regional airports.
goodToKnow: Landlocked country in Central Europe.
Known for its dramatic mountain ranges (High Tatras), national parks, and numerous caves.
Former part of Czechoslovakia, gained independence in 1993.
Member of the European Union, NATO, and the Eurozone.
Temperate continental climate with warm summers and cold, snowy winters.

majorCities.name: Ljubljana
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a charming city known for its vibrant atmosphere, green spaces, and dragon bridge.

majorCities.name: Maribor
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, known for its wine region, oldest vine in the world, and cultural events.

majorCities.name: Celje
majorCities.description: Historic city in central Slovenia, known for its castle and Roman past.

cultureHighlights: Unique Slavic culture with influences from Central European (Austrian, Italian) and Balkan regions.
Strong tradition of folk music (accordion) and dance.
Known for its rich literary tradition and unique Slovene language.
Emphasis on nature, outdoor activities, and environmental consciousness.
Cuisine features a blend of Alpine, Mediterranean, and Pannonian flavors.
transportation: Well-developed road network, including motorways.
Extensive railway system connects major cities.
Efficient public transport in cities (buses).
Ljubljana Jože Pučnik Airport is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Located in Central Europe, with a short Adriatic coastline.
Known for its diverse landscapes, including Julian Alps, karst caves (Postojna, Škocjan), and picturesque lakes (Bled, Bohinj).
One of Europe's most water-rich and forested countries.
Former part of Yugoslavia, gained independence in 1991.
Member of the European Union, NATO, and the Eurozone.

majorCities.name: Honiara
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, located on Guadalcanal island, a major port and administrative center.

cultureHighlights: Diverse Melanesian cultures with numerous distinct languages and customs.
Traditional music (panpipes, drums) and dance are vibrant.
Known for its intricate wood carvings and shell money.
Strong emphasis on customary law, community, and respect for chiefs.
Oral traditions and storytelling are central to cultural transmission.
transportation: Limited road network, mostly unpaved outside Honiara.
No railways.
Inter-island ferries and domestic flights are essential for connecting the islands.
Honiara International Airport is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Island nation in Melanesia, part of Oceania, comprising hundreds of islands.
Known for its pristine coral reefs, diverse marine life, and World War II historical sites.
Faces challenges with infrastructure development and climate change.
Rich in natural resources (timber, fish, minerals).
Tropical wet climate year-round.

majorCities.name: Mogadishu
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a historic port city on the Indian Ocean, rebuilding after conflict.

majorCities.name: Kismayo
majorCities.description: Major port city in southern Somalia.

cultureHighlights: Primarily Somali ethnic group with strong nomadic and Islamic traditions.
Known for its rich oral poetry, which is a central part of cultural expression.
Traditional music often features drums and stringed instruments.
Emphasis on hospitality, family, and clan ties.
Cuisine features camel meat, rice, pasta, and spices.
transportation: Limited and often unpaved road network; travel can be highly unsafe in many areas.
No railways.
Mogadishu Aden Adde International Airport and Hargeisa Egal International Airport are main gateways (status varies).
Air travel is often the most reliable way to move between major cities.
goodToKnow: Located in the Horn of Africa, with the longest coastline on mainland Africa.
Faces ongoing challenges with conflict, political instability, and humanitarian crises.
Vast semi-arid and arid landscapes.
Large diaspora contributes significantly to remittances.
Hot desert climate, with limited rainfall.

majorCities.name: Hargeisa
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a major economic and cultural hub.

majorCities.name: Burao
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, an important livestock and trading center.

cultureHighlights: Primarily Somali ethnic group with strong nomadic and Islamic traditions.
Known for its rich oral poetry, a central part of cultural expression.
Traditional music often features drums and stringed instruments.
Emphasis on hospitality, family, and clan ties.
Cuisine features camel meat, rice, pasta, and spices.
transportation: Road network connects major towns, but many roads are unpaved.
No railways.
Egal International Airport (Hargeisa) is the main international gateway.
Buses and shared taxis are common.
goodToKnow: De facto independent state in the Horn of Africa, but unrecognized internationally.
Relatively stable and peaceful compared to neighboring Somalia.
Primarily semi-arid to arid landscape.
Economy based on livestock, remittances, and limited trade.
Hot desert climate, with limited rainfall.

majorCities.name: Johannesburg
majorCities.description: Largest city and economic powerhouse, a hub of finance, mining, and culture.

majorCities.name: Cape Town
majorCities.description: Legislative capital, known for Table Mountain, beautiful coastline, and vibrant cultural scene.

majorCities.name: Durban
majorCities.description: Major port city on the Indian Ocean, known for its beaches and Indian cultural influence.

majorCities.name: Pretoria
majorCities.description: Administrative capital, known for its jacaranda-lined streets and historic buildings.

majorCities.name: Gqeberha
majorCities.description: Major port city in the Eastern Cape, known for its beaches and automotive industry.

majorCities.name: Bloemfontein
majorCities.description: Judicial capital, located in the Free State province.

majorCities.name: East London
majorCities.description: Coastal city in the Eastern Cape, an important port.

majorCities.name: Polokwane
majorCities.description: Capital of Limpopo province, gateway to northern national parks.

majorCities.name: Kimberley
majorCities.description: Historic city in the Northern Cape, known for its diamond mining history (Big Hole).

cultureHighlights: Extremely diverse 'Rainbow Nation' with 11 official languages and varied ethnic groups.
Rich history of struggle against apartheid, now a vibrant democracy.
Known for its unique music (Kwaito, Amapiano), dance, and literature.
Strong outdoor and sporting culture (rugby, soccer, cricket).
Braai (barbecue) is a central social tradition.
transportation: Well-developed road network, including national highways.
Extensive but often slow railway system (Metrorail for commuters, Shosholoza Meyl for inter-city).
Efficient public transport in major cities (Gautrain in Gauteng).
O. R. Tambo International Airport (Johannesburg) and Cape Town International Airport are major international hubs.
goodToKnow: Located at the southern tip of Africa, with long coastlines on the Atlantic and Indian Oceans.
Incredibly diverse geography, from deserts and savannas to mountains and subtropical coastlines.
Rich in natural resources (gold, diamonds, platinum).
Faces challenges with inequality, unemployment, and crime.
Experiences various climates, from arid to Mediterranean and subtropical.

majorCities.name: Seoul
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a dynamic global metropolis blending ancient palaces with futuristic skyscrapers and K-pop culture.

majorCities.name: Busan
majorCities.description: Second-largest city and major port, known for its beaches, mountains, and fresh seafood.

majorCities.name: Incheon
majorCities.description: Major port city west of Seoul, home to Incheon International Airport and a free economic zone.

majorCities.name: Daegu
majorCities.description: Major city in southeastern South Korea, known for its textile industry and hot summers.

majorCities.name: Daejeon
majorCities.description: Science and technology hub in central South Korea.

majorCities.name: Gwangju
majorCities.description: Major city in southwestern South Korea, known for its democratic history and art.

majorCities.name: Suwon
majorCities.description: Historic city south of Seoul, known for Hwaseong Fortress (UNESCO site) and Samsung.

cultureHighlights: Dynamic modern culture driven by K-pop, K-drama, and K-beauty (Hallyu Wave).
Rich historical heritage (Joseon Dynasty palaces, traditional villages).
Emphasis on respect for elders, education, and collective harmony.
Traditional performing arts (Pansori, Nongak) and martial arts (Taekwondo).
Diverse and flavorful cuisine (Kimchi, Bibimbap, Bulgogi) with a strong fermentation culture.
transportation: Highly efficient and extensive public transport systems (subways, buses) in major cities.
World-class high-speed rail (KTX) connecting major cities.
Well-developed road network, including expressways.
Incheon International Airport is a major global aviation hub.
Ferry services connect to islands (Jeju, Ulleungdo).
goodToKnow: Located on the southern half of the Korean Peninsula in East Asia.
Known for its mountainous landscape, vibrant cities, and technological advancement.
One of the world's most technologically advanced and connected countries.
Temperate climate with four distinct seasons.
Has a strong focus on innovation and economic growth.

majorCities.name: Madrid
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a vibrant metropolis known for its art museums, lively nightlife, and historic squares.

majorCities.name: Barcelona
majorCities.description: Major city in Catalonia, famous for Gaudi's architecture (Sagrada Família), vibrant street life, and beaches.

majorCities.name: Valencia
majorCities.description: Third-largest city, known for its City of Arts and Sciences, futuristic architecture, and Paella.

majorCities.name: Sevilla
majorCities.description: Capital of Andalusia, known for its flamenco, Moorish architecture (Alcázar, Giralda), and vibrant festivals.

majorCities.name: Zaragoza
majorCities.description: City in Aragon, known for its Basilica del Pilar and historical sites.

majorCities.name: Málaga
majorCities.description: Coastal city in Andalusia, on the Costa del Sol, birthplace of Picasso, known for its beaches.

majorCities.name: Murcia
majorCities.description: City in southeastern Spain, known for its cathedral and agricultural region.

majorCities.name: Palma
majorCities.description: Capital of Mallorca island, a major tourist destination with a stunning cathedral and old town.

majorCities.name: Las Palmas de Gran Canaria
majorCities.description: Largest city in the Canary Islands, a major port and tourist hub.

majorCities.name: Bilbao
majorCities.description: Major city in the Basque Country, known for its Guggenheim Museum and modern architecture.

cultureHighlights: Rich history influenced by Roman, Moorish, and Christian cultures.
Diverse regional identities and languages (Castilian Spanish, Catalan, Basque, Galician).
Known for its vibrant festivals (La Tomatina, Running of the Bulls), Flamenco music and dance, and bullfighting.
World-renowned art (Picasso, Goya, Dalí) and architecture (Gaudi).
Tapas culture and a strong emphasis on social life and family.
transportation: Excellent high-speed rail network (AVE) connecting major cities.
Well-developed road network, including motorways (autopistas).
Efficient public transport in cities (metro, trams, buses).

Ferries connect mainland to Balearic Islands, Canary Islands, and North Africa.
goodToKnow: Located in Southwest Europe, occupying most of the Iberian Peninsula, with coastlines on the Atlantic Ocean and Mediterranean Sea.
Diverse geography, including mountains (Pyrenees, Sierra Nevada), central plateau, and coastal plains.
Second largest country in the EU by area.
Popular tourist destination with sunny beaches, historic cities, and diverse cuisine.
Mediterranean climate with hot, dry summers and mild, wet winters; more varied inland.

majorCities.name: Colombo
majorCities.description: The economic capital and largest city, a major port and commercial hub.

majorCities.name: Kandy
majorCities.description: Historic hill city, a UNESCO World Heritage site and home to the Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic.

majorCities.name: Galle
majorCities.description: Historic fort city on the southwest coast, a UNESCO World Heritage site with Dutch colonial architecture.

majorCities.name: Jaffna
majorCities.description: Major city in the northern Tamil-speaking region, known for its temples and cultural heritage.

cultureHighlights: Rich ancient history with influences from Buddhism, Hinduism, and colonial powers.
Theravada Buddhism is central to the culture of the Sinhalese majority.
Traditional performing arts (Kandyan dance) and vibrant festivals (Esala Perahera).
Known for its tea plantations, gemstones, and spices.
Emphasis on hospitality and respect for elders.
transportation: Extensive road network; buses are a primary mode of inter-city transport.
Railway system connects major cities, including scenic routes.
Tuk-tuks are common for local transport in cities.
Bandaranaike International Airport (Colombo) is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Island nation in South Asia, located in the Indian Ocean.
Known as the 'Pearl of the Indian Ocean' for its natural beauty, including beaches, rainforests, and ancient ruins.
Faces ongoing challenges with economic recovery and political stability.
Tropical climate with distinct wet and dry seasons.

majorCities.name: Gustavia
majorCities.description: The capital and main town, a picturesque harbor known for its luxury yachts, boutiques, and Swedish colonial heritage.

cultureHighlights: Unique blend of French, Swedish, and Creole influences.
Known for its upscale tourism and sophisticated atmosphere.
Emphasis on preserving its natural beauty and relaxed, exclusive lifestyle.
Traditional music and dance forms are preserved.
Cuisine features gourmet French dining alongside fresh seafood.
transportation: Limited road network; rental cars and scooters are popular, but roads are narrow and hilly.
Gustaf III Airport (St. Barth) handles small propeller planes only; most arrive via St. Maarten.

goodToKnow: French overseas collectivity in the Caribbean.
Known for its luxurious resorts, pristine beaches, and high-end shopping.
Popular destination for celebrities and yachting enthusiasts.
Experiences a tropical climate with a dry season and a wetter season (hurricane season).
Very high cost of living.

majorCities.name: Marigot
majorCities.description: The capital and main town on the French side, known for its marina, Fort Louis, and market.

cultureHighlights: Blend of French and Caribbean cultures, with influences from various other nationalities.
Shared island with Dutch Sint Maarten, creating a unique cross-cultural environment.
Vibrant music (calypso, soca) and dance scene.
Emphasis on tourism and a lively, festive atmosphere.
Cuisine features French gourmet, Creole, and international dishes.
transportation: Well-developed road network; rental cars and scooters are popular.
Taxis and public buses (minivans) are available.
Grand Case-Espérance Airport (French side) handles regional flights; Princess Juliana International Airport (Dutch side) is the major regional hub.
No internal border controls between French and Dutch sides.
goodToKnow: French overseas collectivity, occupying the northern part of the island of Saint Martin.
Known for its beautiful beaches, relaxed ambiance, and blend of European and Caribbean charm.
Experiences a tropical climate with a hurricane season (June-November).
Duty-free shopping is popular.
Known as the 'Friendly Island'.

majorCities.name: Basseterre
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, located on St. Kitts, known for its colonial architecture and harbor.

majorCities.name: Charlestown
majorCities.description: The main town on Nevis, known for its historic charm and hot springs.

cultureHighlights: Blend of African and British influences, with a strong Creole heritage.
Known for its vibrant Carnival (late December/early January).
Traditional music (calypso, soca, steelpan) and dance.
Emphasis on community spirit and relaxed island life.
Cricket is a national passion.
transportation: Well-developed road network on both islands; rental cars and taxis are available.
Passenger ferry connects St. Kitts and Nevis.
Robert L. Bradshaw International Airport (St. Kitts) and Vance W. Amory International Airport (Nevis) are the main airports.
goodToKnow: Twin-island federation in the West Indies, the smallest sovereign state in the Western Hemisphere.
Known for its lush volcanic landscapes, pristine beaches, and historic plantations.
Tourism and offshore financial services are key economic sectors.
Experiences a tropical climate with a hurricane season (June-November).
Sugar production was historically important.

majorCities.name: Saint-Pierre
majorCities.description: The capital and main town, located on Saint-Pierre island, with a distinct French ambiance.

cultureHighlights: Unique blend of French, Basque, Breton, and Norman traditions, making it distinctly French in North America.
Strong maritime heritage and fishing culture.
Traditional music and dance reflect its French roots.
Emphasis on community, close-knit social life, and preserving its unique identity.
Cuisine features French dishes, fresh seafood, and local specialties.
transportation: Limited road network on the main islands; walking and cycling are common.
Ferries connect Saint-Pierre to Miquelon and mainland Newfoundland (Canada).
Saint-Pierre Airport is the main air gateway, with regional flights to Canada.
goodToKnow: French overseas collectivity in the North Atlantic Ocean, off the coast of Newfoundland, Canada.
Last remaining piece of New France in North America.
Known for its rugged landscapes, colorful houses, and unique cultural blend.
Economy primarily based on fishing, tourism, and French subsidies.
Cool, damp oceanic climate.

majorCities.name: Kingstown
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, located on St. Vincent island, a major port.

cultureHighlights: Blend of African, British, and Indigenous Garifuna influences.
Known for its traditional music (calypso, soca) and dance.
Emphasis on community, relaxed island life, and sailing.
Carnival (Vincy Mas) is a major annual event.
Cuisine features fresh seafood, root vegetables, and callaloo soup.
transportation: Road network on St. Vincent can be winding due to volcanic terrain; rental cars and taxis are available.
Public minibuses (vans) are common.
Inter-island ferries and domestic flights (small planes) are essential for reaching the Grenadines.
Argyle International Airport (St. Vincent) is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Island nation in the Caribbean, comprising the main island of Saint Vincent and the northern Grenadines.
Known for its volcanic origins, lush rainforests, and stunning sailing waters among the Grenadine islands.
The La Soufrière volcano on St. Vincent is active.
Tourism and agriculture are key economic sectors.
Experiences a tropical climate with a hurricane season (June-November).

majorCities.name: Khartoum
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, at the confluence of the Blue and White Nile rivers.

majorCities.name: Omdurman
majorCities.description: Largest city in Sudan, located across the Nile from Khartoum, known for its traditional markets.

majorCities.name: Port Sudan
majorCities.description: Major port city on the Red Sea coast, crucial for trade.

cultureHighlights: Rich history as ancient Nubian kingdoms (Kush, Meroe) and a blend of Arab and African cultures.
Islam is the predominant religion.
Traditional music features drums, stringed instruments (oud), and folk dances.
Emphasis on hospitality, family, and communal gatherings.
Cuisine features fava beans (ful medames), kisra (flatbread), and various stews.
transportation: Limited and often poorly maintained road network; travel can be difficult and unsafe.
Limited railway system, mostly for freight.
River transport on the Nile is important for some areas.
Khartoum International Airport is the main international gateway, but operations are highly disrupted by conflict.
goodToKnow: Located in Northeast Africa, with a Red Sea coastline.
Third largest country in Africa by area.
Faces ongoing severe conflict, humanitarian crises, and political instability.
Known for its ancient pyramids of Meroe and other archaeological sites.
Hot desert to semi-arid climate.

majorCities.name: Paramaribo

cultureHighlights: Highly diverse population with Javanese, Hindustani, African, Indigenous, Chinese, and European influences.

Vibrant music and dance scene (Kawina, Kaseko).
Emphasis on religious tolerance and multicultural coexistence.
Cuisine reflects its diverse ethnic groups (e.g., roti, pom, bami).
transportation: Limited road network, mostly along the coast; interior access is challenging.
No railways.
River transport is crucial for accessing the interior and Amazon rainforest.
Johan Adolf Pengel International Airport is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Located on the northeastern Atlantic coast of South America.
Known for its vast unspoiled rainforest, diverse wildlife, and colonial history.
One of the smallest independent countries in South America.
Rich in natural resources (gold, oil, bauxite).
Tropical rainforest climate.

majorCities.name: Stockholm
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, built on 14 islands, known for its beautiful archipelago, Nobel Prize, and innovation.

majorCities.name: Göteborg

majorCities.name: Malmö
majorCities.description: Third-largest city, located in southern Sweden, known for its modern architecture and multiculturalism.

majorCities.name: Uppsala

majorCities.name: Linköping
