majorCities.name: Helsingborg
majorCities.description: Coastal city in southern Sweden, with a historic tower and ferry connection to Denmark.

cultureHighlights: Strong emphasis on equality, social welfare, and consensus ('lagom' - just right).
Known for its design (IKEA, H&M), music (ABBA, pop), and innovation.
Traditions like Midsummer, Lucia, and Fika (coffee break) are important.
Deep connection to nature and outdoor activities.
Royal family is a cultural symbol.
transportation: Excellent public transport (trains, buses, metro in Stockholm) and well-developed road network.
Extensive railway system connects major cities, including high-speed trains.
Stockholm Arlanda Airport is the main international gateway, with other regional airports.
Ferries connect to neighboring countries and islands.
goodToKnow: Nordic country in Northern Europe, part of the Scandinavian Peninsula, with a long Baltic Sea coastline.
Vast forests, numerous lakes, and Arctic landscapes in the north.
High standard of living and strong social safety net.
Constitutional monarchy.
Temperate climate in the south, subarctic in the north.

majorCities.name: Zurich
majorCities.description: Largest city and financial hub, known for its high quality of life, beautiful lake, and vibrant arts scene.

majorCities.name: Geneva
majorCities.description: Global diplomatic hub, home to many international organizations, located on Lake Geneva.

majorCities.name: Basel
majorCities.description: Major cultural and pharmaceutical hub on the Rhine River, bordering France and Germany.

majorCities.name: Lausanne
majorCities.description: City on Lake Geneva, home to the International Olympic Committee and a vibrant student population.

majorCities.name: Bern
majorCities.description: The de facto capital, a charming medieval city and UNESCO World Heritage site.

majorCities.name: Luzern
majorCities.description: Picturesque city on Lake Lucerne, known for its Chapel Bridge and mountain views.

cultureHighlights: Diverse regional cultures and languages (German, French, Italian) due to its unique federal structure.

Known for its chocolate, cheese, watches, and banking.
Alpine traditions and outdoor sports (skiing, hiking) are integral.
Traditional folk music (yodeling, alphorn) and festivals.

Well-maintained road network.

High level of integration between different modes of transport.

High standard of living and strong economy.
Direct democracy system.
Neutrality in international affairs.
Alpine climate with cold, snowy winters and warm summers.

majorCities.name: Damascus
majorCities.description: The capital and one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world, with immense historical significance.

majorCities.name: Aleppo
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, a historic trading hub, severely affected by conflict.

majorCities.name: Homs
majorCities.description: Major city in central Syria, also heavily impacted by conflict.

majorCities.name: Hama
majorCities.description: City in central Syria, known for its ancient waterwheels (norias).

majorCities.name: Latakia
majorCities.description: Major port city on the Mediterranean coast.

cultureHighlights: Ancient civilization with a rich history of Roman, Byzantine, Islamic, and Ottoman influences.
Arabic is the predominant language, with diverse ethnic and religious groups.
Traditional music and dance forms (Dabke, Sufi whirling).
Emphasis on hospitality, poetry, and storytelling.
Cuisine features Levantine dishes like kibbeh, hummus, and tabbouleh.
transportation: Road network connects major cities, but conditions and safety are highly compromised due to ongoing conflict.
Limited and disrupted railway system.
Public transport is severely affected by the conflict.
Damascus International Airport (operations vary due to conflict).
goodToKnow: Located in Western Asia, with a Mediterranean coastline.

Diverse landscapes from coastal plains to mountains and deserts.

majorCities.name: São Tomé

Emphasis on relaxed island life and strong community ties.

Experiences various climates, from tropical to arid, temperate, and subarctic.
Known for its national parks and diverse natural wonders.

majorCities.name: Montevideo
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a vibrant port city on the Río de la Plata, known for its colonial architecture and beaches.

majorCities.name: Salto
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, located on the Uruguay River, an important agricultural and industrial center.

cultureHighlights: Strong European heritage, particularly Italian and Spanish, with influences from indigenous and African cultures.
Known for its Tango music and dance (shared with Argentina) and Candombe (Afro-Uruguayan rhythm).
Emphasis on football (soccer) as a national passion.
Mate tea is a central part of social life.
Carnival is a major annual celebration.
transportation: Well-developed road network connecting major cities.
Limited railway system, mostly for freight.
Public buses are common for inter-city travel.
Carrasco International Airport (Montevideo) is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Located in southeastern South America, bordered by Brazil and Argentina, with a coastline on the Atlantic Ocean.
One of the most politically and economically stable countries in Latin America.
Known for its rolling hills, fertile plains, and extensive coastline with beaches.
High literacy rate and strong social welfare system.
Temperate climate with distinct seasons.

majorCities.name: Port Vila
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, located on Efate island, a major port and economic center.

majorCities.name: Luganville
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, located on Espiritu Santo island, known for its diving sites.

cultureHighlights: Diverse Melanesian cultures with over 100 indigenous languages.
Customary law and traditional ways of life are strong in many communities.
Vibrant traditional music and dance forms.
Known for its unique kava culture (traditional drink).
Nagol (land diving) on Pentecost Island is a unique ritual.
transportation: Limited road networks on the main islands; quality varies.
No railways.
Inter-island flights are essential for connecting the islands.
Bauerfield International Airport (Port Vila) is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Island nation in Melanesia, part of Oceania, comprising about 80 islands.
Known for its active volcanoes, pristine beaches, and rich marine life (especially dive sites).
Experiences a tropical climate with a wet and dry season; prone to cyclones and earthquakes.
Economy primarily based on agriculture, tourism, and offshore financial services.
Bislama (a Pidgin English) is the national language.

majorCities.name: Vatican City
majorCities.description: The smallest independent state in the world, home to the Pope and a treasure trove of art and architecture.

cultureHighlights: Center of the Catholic Church, with profound religious and artistic significance.
Home to iconic religious and artistic masterpieces (St. Peter's Basilica, Sistine Chapel).
Unique diplomatic and ecclesiastical traditions.
The Pontifical Swiss Guard serves as the military force.
Latin is an official language, used for formal documents.
transportation: Pedestrian-friendly; no public transport within the city state.
Connected to Rome's public transport network (metro, bus) at its borders.
No airport; closest major airport is Rome Fiumicino Airport (Italy).
goodToKnow: Enclaved microstate within Rome, Italy.
Smallest sovereign state in the world by both area and population.
The Pope is the absolute monarch.
A UNESCO World Heritage site in its entirety.
Receives numerous pilgrims and tourists annually.

majorCities.name: Caracas
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, located in a valley, a major cultural and economic center.

majorCities.name: Maracaibo
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, a major oil-producing city on Lake Maracaibo.

majorCities.name: Valencia
majorCities.description: Major industrial city in central Venezuela.

majorCities.name: Barquisimeto
majorCities.description: City in west-central Venezuela, known for its music and handicrafts.

majorCities.name: Ciudad Guayana
majorCities.description: Planned city in southeastern Venezuela, a major industrial and mining center.

cultureHighlights: Blend of Indigenous, Spanish, and African influences.
Known for its vibrant music (joropo, salsa) and dance.
Baseball is a national passion.
Emphasis on family, hospitality, and communal gatherings.
Cuisine features arepas (corn cakes), pabellón criollo (national dish), and tropical fruits.
transportation: Road network connects major cities, but conditions vary; travel can be challenging and unsafe in some areas.
Limited railway system.
Caracas Metro is efficient; taxis and shared taxis (por puestos) are common.
Simón Bolívar International Airport (Caracas) is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Located on the northern coast of South America, with a Caribbean coastline.
Home to Angel Falls (world's highest waterfall) and diverse natural landscapes (Andes mountains, Amazon basin, Llanos plains).
Rich in oil reserves, which dominate its economy but have led to economic crises.
Faces ongoing severe economic, political, and humanitarian crises.
Tropical climate with distinct wet and dry seasons.

majorCities.name: Ho Chi Minh City
majorCities.description: Largest city and economic hub in the south, a bustling metropolis with a mix of historical and modern architecture.

majorCities.name: Hanoi
majorCities.description: The capital, a historic city in the north known for its Old Quarter, lakes, and French colonial architecture.

majorCities.name: Da Nang
majorCities.description: Major coastal city in central Vietnam, known for its beaches and proximity to historical sites (Hoi An, Hue).

majorCities.name: Can Tho
majorCities.description: Largest city in the Mekong Delta, known for its floating markets and river life.

majorCities.name: Hai Phong
majorCities.description: Major port city in northern Vietnam, an important industrial and economic center.

majorCities.name: Bien Hoa
majorCities.description: Industrial city near Ho Chi Minh City.

majorCities.name: Hue
majorCities.description: Former imperial capital in central Vietnam, a UNESCO World Heritage site with ancient palaces and tombs.

cultureHighlights: Rich history spanning thousands of years, with influences from China, France, and other Southeast Asian cultures.
Strong emphasis on family, community, and ancestral worship.
Traditional performing arts (water puppetry, Cai Luong opera).
Known for its delicious and diverse cuisine (Pho, Banh Mi, spring rolls).
Vibrant festivals (Tet Lunar New Year) and traditional crafts (silk, lacquerware).
transportation: Extensive road network; motorbikes are a primary mode of personal transport.
Railway system runs north-south (Reunification Express).
Public buses are available; taxis and ride-sharing apps are common in cities.
Noi Bai International Airport (Hanoi) and Tan Son Nhat International Airport (Ho Chi Minh City) are major international gateways.
goodToKnow: Located in Southeast Asia, with a long coastline on the South China Sea.
Diverse geography, including mountainous regions, deltas (Mekong, Red River), and a long, narrow shape.
Rapidly growing economy and popular tourist destination.
Tropical monsoon climate with wet and dry seasons.

majorCities.name: Laayoune
majorCities.description: Largest city, controlled by Morocco, serves as the de facto capital of Moroccan-administered Western Sahara.

majorCities.name: Dakhla
majorCities.description: Coastal city, controlled by Morocco, known for its fishing and watersports.

cultureHighlights: Predominantly Sahrawi Bedouin culture, with influences from Arab and Berber traditions.
Strong emphasis on nomadic traditions, hospitality, and traditional music.
Oral traditions and poetry are central to cultural expression.
Islam is the predominant religion.
Traditional tea ceremony is an important social ritual.
transportation: Limited road network, mostly unpaved outside main towns; travel restrictions and landmines exist.
No railways.
Laayoune Airport (controlled by Morocco) serves the region, but international access is complex.
Travel is highly restricted due to the ongoing conflict.
goodToKnow: Disputed territory in North Africa, largely controlled by Morocco, with a portion controlled by the Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic (Polisario Front).
Mostly desert landscape (Sahara Desert).
Economy primarily based on phosphate mining and fishing in Moroccan-controlled areas; Polisario-controlled areas are largely desert and refugee camps.
Long-standing political conflict and UN peacekeeping mission.
Hot, arid desert climate.

majorCities.name: Mata-Utu
majorCities.description: The capital and largest settlement, located on Wallis Island, with a royal palace and cathedral.

cultureHighlights: Rich Polynesian culture, with strong traditional customs and three indigenous kingdoms.
Traditional music and dance (sasa) are central to community events.
Emphasis on respect for chiefs, family, and communal living.
Known for its tapa cloth and weaving.
Catholicism is the predominant religion.
transportation: Limited road network on the main islands; walking and cycling are common.
No railways.
Wallis Island Airport is the main airport, with limited regional flights.
Inter-island boat services are available.
goodToKnow: French overseas collectivity in Polynesia, South Pacific Ocean.
Comprises three main islands: Wallis, Futuna, and Alofi, with several smaller islets.
Known for its pristine lagoons, volcanic landscapes, and preserved traditional ways of life.
Economy is primarily based on subsistence agriculture, fishing, and French subsidies.
Tropical marine climate.

YEM.capital: Sanaa (de facto Houthi), Aden (de facto Yemeni gov.)

majorCities.name: Sanaa
majorCities.description: The historic capital, one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world, with unique mud-brick architecture (UNESCO site).

majorCities.name: Aden
majorCities.description: Major port city and economic hub on the Gulf of Aden, currently the de facto capital for the internationally recognized government.

majorCities.name: Taiz
majorCities.description: Third-largest city, located in the mountainous interior, heavily impacted by conflict.

majorCities.name: Hodeidah
majorCities.description: Major port city on the Red Sea, a crucial entry point for humanitarian aid.

cultureHighlights: Rich ancient history and unique Arabian culture, with a strong tribal heritage.
Islam is the predominant religion.
Known for its distinctive architecture (mud-brick skyscrapers, fortified villages) and traditional qat chewing.
Traditional music and dance are prominent.
Emphasis on hospitality, poetry, and storytelling.
transportation: Road network connects major cities, but conditions and safety are extremely poor due to ongoing conflict.
No railways.
Major airports exist but operations are highly restricted and unreliable due to conflict.
Travel is extremely dangerous and often impossible for civilians and foreigners.
goodToKnow: Located on the southern end of the Arabian Peninsula, with coastlines on the Red Sea and Arabian Sea.
Faces severe ongoing civil war, humanitarian crises, and widespread famine.
Diverse geography, including arid plains, rugged mountains, and desert plateaus.
One of the poorest countries in the Arab world.
Arid to semi-arid climate.

majorCities.name: Lusaka
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a rapidly growing urban center in central Zambia.

majorCities.name: Kitwe
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, a major mining city in the Copperbelt Province.

majorCities.name: Ndola
majorCities.description: Third-largest city, also in the Copperbelt, an important industrial and commercial hub.

majorCities.name: Livingstone
majorCities.description: Major tourist town in southern Zambia, gateway to Victoria Falls.

cultureHighlights: Diverse ethnic groups (Bemba, Nyanja, Tonga, Lozi) with rich traditions of music and dance.
Known for its vibrant traditional ceremonies and festivals.
Emphasis on hospitality, community, and respect for elders.
Traditional crafts include basket weaving and wood carving.
Cuisine features Nshima (cornmeal porridge) as a staple, with various relishes.
transportation: Road network connects major towns, but quality varies; buses are common for inter-city travel.
Railway system connects Lusaka to Copperbelt and Tanzania (TAZARA).
Domestic flights connect major cities and national parks.
Kenneth Kaunda International Airport (Lusaka) is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Landlocked country in Southern Africa.
Known for its vast wilderness, abundant wildlife, and the majestic Victoria Falls (shared with Zimbabwe).
Rich in copper resources, which dominate its economy.
Experiences a tropical climate, with wet and dry seasons.
Faces challenges with poverty and infrastructure development.

majorCities.name: Luxembourg
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a major financial center with a historic old town (UNESCO/cultural).

majorCities.name: Esch-sur-Alzette
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, located in the industrial south, part of the former steel region.

majorCities.name: Differdange
majorCities.description: Growing southern city, known for its university campus and proximity to the French border.

cultureHighlights: Multilingual and multicultural society with strong European identity.
Blend of Germanic and Latin cultural influences.
Vibrant traditions of classical music, theater, and modern art.
Celebrates National Day on June 23 with major festivities.
Cuisine reflects French and German influences; known for wines and pastries.
transportation: Excellent road and rail connections across the country and to neighboring countries.
Public transportation is free nationwide (trains, buses, trams).
Luxembourg Airport connects to major European hubs.
Cycling and walking infrastructure is well-developed.
goodToKnow: Small landlocked country in Western Europe, bordered by Belgium, France, and Germany.
One of the wealthiest countries in the world per capita.
Founding member of the EU and key host of European institutions.
UNESCO-listed Old Quarters and Fortifications of Luxembourg City.
Mild temperate climate with cool winters and moderate summers.

majorCities.name: Macau
majorCities.description: Densely populated Special Administrative Region of China, famous for its casinos, colonial heritage, and unique East-meets-West culture.

cultureHighlights: Blend of Chinese and Portuguese heritage, with strong cultural preservation efforts.
UNESCO-listed Historic Centre of Macau includes churches, fortresses, and traditional Chinese temples.
Known as the 'Las Vegas of Asia' due to its booming casino industry.
Cantonese opera, dragon boat races, and Portuguese festivals are celebrated.
Macanese cuisine blends Chinese and Portuguese flavors.
transportation: Extensive public bus system and new light rail line (Macau LRT).
Ferries connect to Hong Kong and mainland China.
Macau International Airport handles regional flights.
Walking is convenient due to compact size; taxis widely available.
goodToKnow: Special Administrative Region of China, located on the southern coast facing the South China Sea.
Former Portuguese colony, returned to China in 1999 under the 'One Country, Two Systems' framework.
Major tourism and gaming hub with one of the world's highest GDPs per capita.
Subtropical climate with hot summers and mild winters.
Uses traditional Chinese characters and drives on the left.

majorCities.name: Antananarivo
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, located in the central highlands, known for its colonial architecture and political significance.

majorCities.name: Toamasina
majorCities.description: Largest port city on the east coast, a hub for trade and transportation.

majorCities.name: Antsirabe
majorCities.description: Highland city known for its cooler climate, thermal baths, and colonial charm.

majorCities.name: Fianarantsoa
majorCities.description: Cultural and academic center in the southern highlands, gateway to national parks.

majorCities.name: Mahajanga
majorCities.description: Major coastal city in the northwest, known for its beaches and relaxed atmosphere.

cultureHighlights: Unique blend of African, Austronesian, and French influences.
Rich oral traditions, music (hiragasy), and traditional ceremonies (famadihana).
Strong belief in ancestral worship and respect for nature.
Famous for crafts including woodcarving, textiles, and jewelry.
Cuisine includes rice-based dishes, zebu meat, and tropical fruits.
transportation: Roads connect major cities but are often in poor condition.
No active railways for passenger transport (only limited freight lines).
Public minibuses (taxi-brousse) are common for intercity travel.
Ivato International Airport (Antananarivo) is the main gateway.
Domestic flights serve remote areas and tourist destinations.
goodToKnow: Island nation off the southeast coast of Africa, fourth-largest island in the world.
Renowned for its unique biodiversity—over 90% of species are endemic.
Home to lemurs, baobab trees, and diverse ecosystems (rainforests, dry forests, highlands).
Faces challenges with deforestation, poverty, and infrastructure.
Tropical climate with regional variations and distinct wet/dry seasons.

majorCities.name: Harare
majorCities.description: The capital and largest city, a major commercial and administrative hub.

majorCities.name: Bulawayo
majorCities.description: Second-largest city, a historic industrial center in southwestern Zimbabwe.

majorCities.name: Chitungwiza
majorCities.description: Major high-density dormitory town south of Harare.

majorCities.name: Mutare
majorCities.description: Third-largest city, located in eastern Zimbabwe, close to the Mozambican border.

cultureHighlights: Diverse ethnic groups (Shona, Ndebele) with rich traditions of music and dance (Mbira music).
Known for its traditional stone sculpture (Shona sculpture).
Emphasis on hospitality, community, and respect for ancestors.
Strong oral traditions and storytelling.
Cuisine features Sadza (cornmeal porridge) as a staple, with various stews.
transportation: Road network connects major towns, but quality varies; buses and kombis (minibuses) are common.
Limited railway system, often unreliable.
Domestic flights connect major cities and tourist destinations.
Robert Gabriel Mugabe International Airport (Harare) is the main international gateway.
goodToKnow: Landlocked country in Southern Africa.
Known for its diverse wildlife, national parks, and the majestic Victoria Falls (shared with Zambia).
Rich in mineral resources (gold, platinum, diamonds).
Faces ongoing severe economic challenges and political instability.
Experiences a tropical climate, moderated by altitude.

majorCities.name: Mariehamn
majorCities.description: The capital and only city, located on Fasta Åland, a charming port town with maritime heritage.

cultureHighlights: Unique autonomous region with a distinct Swedish culture within Finland.
Strong maritime heritage and seafaring traditions.
Emphasis on local self-governance and preservation of nature.
Traditional folk music and festivals.
Known for its apple orchards and local produce.
transportation: Well-maintained road network on Fasta Åland; cycling is popular.
No railways.
Mariehamn Airport is the main airport, with regional flights to Finland and Sweden.
Extensive ferry services connect to Finland, Sweden, and Estonia.
goodToKnow: Autonomous region of Finland, an archipelago located in the Baltic Sea.
Predominantly Swedish-speaking population.
Demilitarized zone with a unique self-governing status.
Known for its beautiful islands, red granite rocks, and charming small towns.
Cool temperate climate.
